name: The Credit Agents
description_short: The Credit Agents is a Houston, TX-based credit repair firm with locations in Houston, Frisco, and Pflugerville. Not BBB accredited or rated. 2,755 Google reviews. Mixed customer feedback.
description_long:
The Credit Agents is a credit repair and financial consulting firm headquartered in Houston, Texas, with additional locations in Frisco and Pflugerville. Founded around 2017, the company focuses on helping Texas consumers improve their credit scores through professional dispute services with all three credit bureaus. The company has accumulated approximately 2,755 Google reviews, building a strong local presence in the Texas credit repair market.

Services include credit report analysis across Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion, dispute letter preparation and filing, debt validation requests, credit-building guidance, and financial consulting. The company's pricing is available on their website but specific details require direct inquiry. The multi-location Texas footprint provides in-person consultation options, which is a genuine advantage for consumers who prefer face-to-face interaction over remote-only services.

The Credit Agents is not BBB accredited and has no BBB rating, which limits independent quality verification. Customer reviews are mixed — some clients report meaningful credit score improvements and praise customer service, while others report paying significant fees ($1,000+) with no results over 6+ months and no communication from the company. This wide variance in reported experiences is unfortunately common in the credit repair industry and underscores the importance of understanding contract terms, expected timelines, and cancellation policies before enrolling.

In the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: 6-month conditional warranty: clients who complete 6 months as active participants and receive zero deleted items may request a full refund. Partial results do not qualify.

pros: Pay-per-deletion model — clients only pay when items are actually removed from their report
4.7/5 rating from approximately 2,755 reviews across third-party platforms
6-month money-back warranty if zero items are deleted after completing the program
Works with all three major bureaus: Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion
Dedicated credit analyst assigned to each client for personalized dispute management
FDCPA attorney network available for complex items like bankruptcies and judgments
Free initial credit review with no obligation to enroll
cons: ~$349 setup fee required upfront before any dispute work begins, regardless of eventual results
Per-deletion pricing schedule is not publicly disclosed — total program cost is unpredictable without a direct quote
Six-month warranty is conditional on zero deletions; clients with partial results receive no refund
Not BBB accredited; BBB has insufficient information to issue a rating, a transparency gap for cautious consumers
No verified industry certifications (NACSO, NFCC, HUD) found on website or in public records
best_for: Texas residents seeking a local credit repair provider with multiple office locations
Consumers with credit report errors needing professional dispute services
Individuals in the Houston metro area preferring in-person credit repair consultation
People preparing for home or auto purchases in Texas who need credit improvement
diagnosis: The Credit Agents benefits from multiple Texas locations and local market presence, but the lack of BBB accreditation and mixed reviews (some reporting $1,000+ paid with no results) are concerning. Without independent credentialing or transparent pricing, consumers take on risk. Before enrolling: get pricing in writing, understand the cancellation policy, set clear timeline expectations, and compare against nationally-established providers. Texas consumers may also consider local BBB-accredited firms or national options like The Credit People ($79/mo) or Sky Blue Credit.
services: Identity fraud and inaccurate personal information disputes
Home loan application assistance (mortgage ambassador service)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
